Title: Flow-based generative models for Markov chain Monte Carlo in lattice field theory
Problem: LFT sampling near criticality is slowed by local MCMC.
MechanismCorrection: A normalizing flow supplies independent proposals; an independence Metropolis-Hastings chain supplies asymptotic exactness.
Result: In 2D phi4, observables agree with HMC/local Metropolis and rejection-driven correlations are reduced.
Limitation: Proof of principle in the symmetric phase; architecture search and training-inclusive cost optimization are left open.

Title: Equivariant flow-based sampling for lattice gauge theory
Problem: Topological freezing obstructs conventional sampling in lattice gauge theory.
MechanismCorrection: Gauge-equivariant coupling layers preserve symmetry and feed an exact Metropolis-Hastings chain.
Result: For 2D U(1), flow proposals traverse topological sectors more readily than HMC/heat bath at the studied coupling.
Limitation: The demonstration is a low-dimensional solvable gauge theory, not a cost-matched 4D QCD result.

Title: Estimation of Thermodynamic Observables in Lattice Field Theories with Deep Generative Models
Problem: Absolute free energies are difficult for standard MCMC integration paths.
MechanismCorrection: A tractable deep generative density enables direct free-energy and thermodynamic estimators with importance weighting.
Result: The 2D phi4 tests agree with reference calculations and avoid parameter-path integration.
Limitation: Estimator quality depends on overlap/model quality; the paper does not establish large-volume cost scaling.

Title: Flow-based sampling for fermionic lattice field theories
Problem: Fermion determinants and pseudofermion structure complicate learned LFT proposals.
MechanismCorrection: Joint/auxiliary flow constructions model scalar and pseudofermion variables, followed by exact MCMC correction.
Result: A 2D Yukawa theory demonstrates viable fermionic flow-based sampling and correct observables.
Limitation: The benchmark is two dimensional; determinant/pseudofermion cost and scaling to realistic dynamical fermions remain unresolved.

Title: Flow-based sampling for multimodal and extended-mode distributions in lattice field theory
Problem: Reverse-KL flows can miss separated or extended modes.
MechanismCorrection: Adiabatic retraining, flow-distance regularization, symmetry-aware models, and composite flow/MCMC transitions repair mode coverage.
Result: Real and complex scalar tests show that composite samplers can remain useful where a pure flow fails.
Limitation: No single intervention guarantees coverage; diagnostics and training choices remain model dependent.

Title: Scaling Up Machine Learning For Quantum Field Theory with Equivariant Continuous Flows
Problem: Generic deep flows scale poorly and neglect field-theory symmetry.
MechanismCorrection: A shallow equivariant continuous flow uses domain-specific basis functions and MH correction.
Result: Relative ESS rises from about 1% for the RealNVP baseline to 66% in the reported phi4 setting.
Limitation: Systematic scalability assessment is explicitly future work.

Title: Path Integral Sampler: a stochastic control approach for sampling
Problem: Sampling unnormalized targets remains hard for finite-step variational samplers.
MechanismCorrection: Path Integral Sampler learns a stochastic-control drift and uses trajectory importance weights for calibration.
Result: Benchmarks compare favorably with contemporary sampling methods across multimodal targets.
Limitation: Neural training adds overhead and finite-step quality depends on optimization and hyperparameters.

Title: Stochastic normalizing flows as non-equilibrium transformations
Problem: Free-energy estimation and efficient transport both suffer from nonequilibrium dissipation.
MechanismCorrection: Stochastic normalizing flows interleave trainable invertible layers with stochastic updates under a Jarzynski/work framework.
Result: Scalar-field examples show reduced work fluctuations and improved free-energy/sampling efficiency over simpler protocols.
Limitation: Benefits depend on protocol/training design and the paper does not establish general high-dimensional cost scaling.

Title: Flow-based sampling in the lattice Schwinger model at criticality
Problem: At criticality conventional chains can fail to visit all sectors in the lattice Schwinger model.
MechanismCorrection: A symmetry-aware flow proposes global configurations inside an exact MCMC construction.
Result: The flow-based chain samples both critical sectors and yields uncertainties missed by stuck conventional chains.
Limitation: The result is model-specific and end-to-end training/generation cost is not matched against alternatives.

Title: Learning lattice quantum field theories with equivariant continuous flows
Problem: LFT samplers need symmetry, efficiency, and transfer across parameters/volumes.
MechanismCorrection: An equivariant neural ODE defines a continuous normalizing flow with exact MH correction.
Result: It outperforms earlier flows in phi4 and demonstrates joint-theory learning plus transfer to larger lattices.
Limitation: Evidence remains centered on 2D scalar theory; transfer does not by itself prove asymptotic cost scaling.

Title: Stochastic normalizing flows for lattice field theory
Problem: Pure deterministic flows and pure stochastic protocols each face efficiency limits.
MechanismCorrection: Stochastic normalizing flows combine invertible layers and stochastic evolution with Jarzynski work accounting.
Result: 2D phi4 examples show the hybrid can lower dissipation and improve partition-function estimation.
Limitation: This proceedings treatment is compact and leaves broader scaling/performance comparisons open.

Title: Denoising Diffusion Samplers
Problem: Diffusion sampling is needed when target samples are unavailable but an unnormalized density is evaluable.
MechanismCorrection: DDS minimizes a path-space KL for a learned reverse diffusion and evaluates discretized trajectory importance weights.
Result: Across synthetic and statistical benchmarks DDS competes with AIS, SMC, and PIS.
Limitation: Discretization and optimization remain consequential; bespoke schedules and stable training are unresolved in some cases.

Title: Diffusion models as stochastic quantization in lattice field theory
Problem: Critical slowing down raises the cost of generating LFT ensembles.
MechanismCorrection: A supervised score model reverses Langevin noising; likelihood evaluation permits an MH-corrected diffusion proposal chain.
Result: In 2D phi4, generated observables agree well and the MH chain reduces autocorrelation near the studied critical region.
Limitation: It requires reference configurations for training and does not establish training-inclusive scaling to larger dimensions.

Title: NETS: A Non-Equilibrium Transport Sampler
Problem: Unnormalized high-dimensional and multimodal targets require transport with controlled weights.
MechanismCorrection: NETS learns an additional drift in a nonequilibrium SDE and tracks work/importance weights; diffusivity can be tuned after training.
Result: Benchmarks, including lattice phi4, report strong ESS and sampling performance relative to selected baselines.
Limitation: Weight variance and learned-drift accuracy remain gates; cost matching across training and generation is incomplete.

Title: Exploring Generative Networks for Manifolds with Non-Trivial Topology
Problem: Connected invertible/continuous generators can fail on disconnected or nontrivial topology.
MechanismCorrection: A GFlowNet-inspired sequential construction uses trajectory balance to explore multiple topological components.
Result: Triple-ring and 2D scalar examples show improved exploration of separated structures.
Limitation: Proceedings-scale evidence and toy/scalar tests do not establish gauge-theory exactness or cost scaling.

Title: Physics-conditioned diffusion models for lattice gauge theory
Problem: Gauge-field sampling faces critical and topological freezing.
MechanismCorrection: A physics-conditioned gauge diffusion model is paired with Metropolis-adjusted Langevin dynamics and supports lattice-size transfer.
Result: 2D U(1) tests reproduce observables and improve topological exploration relative to conventional baselines.
Limitation: The study remains low-dimensional and does not settle four-dimensional non-Abelian cost or training-data demands.

Title: Combining complex Langevin dynamics with score-based and energy-based diffusion models
Problem: Complex weights create sign/overlap failures for importance sampling.
MechanismCorrection: Score- and energy-based models learn the stationary complex-Langevin distribution and diagnose its tails/correctness conditions.
Result: A complex quartic model shows agreement and exposes how diffusion models represent the stationary distribution.
Limitation: The evidence is a low-dimensional complex model and does not solve finite-density lattice QCD.

Title: Group-Equivariant Diffusion Models for Lattice Field Theory
Problem: Diffusion models for LQFT must respect group symmetry and support exact validation.
MechanismCorrection: Group-equivariant score networks use force-regularized score matching; likelihood/acceptance diagnostics provide correction checks.
Result: Phi4 and 2D U(1) experiments reproduce observables and show the value of symmetry-aware models.
Limitation: Results are on scalar and 2D Abelian systems; non-Abelian/high-dimensional scaling is left open.

Title: Variational Autoregressive Networks Applied to $\phi^4$ Field Theory Systems
Problem: Data-free autoregressive proposals can retain residual bias and training burden.
MechanismCorrection: A VAN is trained variationally and augmented with single-site/block MH corrections plus parameter/lattice transfer tests.
Result: Ising and phi4 observables agree with MC in the explored range and no clear critical slowing is seen there.
Limitation: The explored models/sizes are limited; transfer and absence of observed slowing do not prove asymptotic scaling.

Title: Generalizable Equivariant Diffusion Models for Non-Abelian Lattice Gauge Theory
Problem: Non-Abelian gauge diffusion models must generalize beyond one training ensemble.
MechanismCorrection: Gauge-equivariant L-CNN scores feed a Metropolis-adjusted annealed Langevin algorithm.
Result: 2D U(2)/SU(2) tests transfer across inverse couplings and lattice sizes with modest accuracy loss and moderate acceptance.
Limitation: Training is supervised and evidence is two dimensional; 4D scaling remains untested.

Title: Diffusion Models for SU(2) Lattice Gauge Theory in Two Dimensions
Problem: SU(2) gauge sampling suffers conventional autocorrelation and topology challenges.
MechanismCorrection: A group-aware score model learns reverse diffusion on gauge configurations generated for training.
Result: In 2D SU(2), generated observables are compared with HMC and show viable ensemble generation.
Limitation: The study is 2D and supervised; exact correction and scaling evidence are less developed than in MH-adjusted approaches.

Title: Scalable Generative Sampling and Multilevel Estimation for Lattice Field Theories Near Criticality
Problem: Near-critical LFT sampling becomes inefficient at large volume.
MechanismCorrection: A renormalization-inspired coarse-to-fine hierarchy combines conditional Gaussian mixtures and masked continuous flows; exact restrictions enable MLMC.
Result: Critical 2D phi4 tests report much shorter autocorrelation than HMC, high importance efficiency, and unbiased MLMC observables.
Limitation: Extension to nontrivial global/topological structure and realistic gauge/fermion systems remains future work.

Title: Diffusion model for SU(N) gauge theories
Problem: SU(N) gauge-field sampling slows toward fine lattice spacing.
MechanismCorrection: Implicit score matching is formulated on group manifolds and used in reverse diffusion/HMD-based sampling.
Result: SU(3) examples in 2D and 4D reproduce several Wilson-action observables against HMC.
Limitation: Sampling efficiency degrades in harder regimes and the authors list architecture/training improvements; general cost superiority is not shown.

Title: Operator Spectroscopy of Trained Lattice Samplers
Problem: Observable agreement alone does not identify what a trained sampler learned or where its residual bias lives.
MechanismCorrection: Predeclared lattice-operator bases project learned velocity/score/action residuals with held-out and wrong-sector controls.
Result: Across phi4 flows/diffusions/NFs and gauge teachers, projections isolate zero-mode, finite-k, and symmetry-resolved residual sectors.
Limitation: Operator bases are model/symmetry dependent; class transfer does not imply coefficient transfer or corrected sampling.

Title: The critical slowing down in diffusion models
Problem: It is not guaranteed that diffusion models eliminate critical slowing down.
MechanismCorrection: The Gaussian O(n to infinity) limit permits an analytic study of score-learning and generation-time scaling for one- and two-layer local architectures.
Result: A one-layer model inherits critical slowing, while depth/locality changes training scaling from quadratic to logarithmic in system size in the controlled setting.
Limitation: The exact analysis is a Gaussian large-n limit, so interacting finite-n and gauge conclusions require new tests.

Title: Stochastic Path Sampler For Lattice Field Theory
Problem: Critical slowing makes standard LFT sampling inefficient near transitions/continuum limits.
MechanismCorrection: SPS learns forward/backward stochastic paths by path-space variational free energy and corrects independent proposals with extended-space IMH.
Result: For Lx8 2D phi4 through L=64, corrected observables agree with HMC; at kappa=0.27 tau_int is about 0.5 IMH steps versus about 160 HMC trajectories.
Limitation: Units are not cost matched; acceptance falls with volume, each (kappa,L) is trained separately, and the architecture grows globally with L.

Title: Diffusion Models for Sampling Near Criticality in Lattice Field Theories
Problem: Near-critical learned sampling needs diagnostics and credible transfer to unseen volume.
MechanismCorrection: A fully convolutional diffusion score shared across volumes is tested with local drift diagnostics, MALA acceptance, and HMC-referenced ESS.
Result: 2D transfer matches or improves in-distribution models; a 3D model trained on L=4,8,16,32 transfers to L=64 for most observables.
Limitation: Residual zero-mode/action-density bias remains, including a broken-phase susceptibility excess in 3D.
